name: openpaw
description: Personal Assistant Wizard for Claude Code — npx pawmode

commit.message: refine paw interface palette and startup copy
document paw setup opt-in and status line
add paw style opt-in to setup wizard
add lock-in status line to paw theme
document theme install and verify commands
add install and verify flow for paw theme
document native Claude Code paw fallback
add native paw mascot fallback for Claude Code
fix configure menu indentation
add paw theme support for Claude Code
fix typecheck blockers in setup and lockin

Add quick actions, standup export, confirm dialogs, focus indicator

- Quick actions bar: Export Standup, Clear Done, Sort by Priority
- GET /api/standup endpoint — generates markdown standup report
- DELETE /api/tasks/done — batch clear completed tasks
- Confirm dialog before task deletion (prevents accidental deletes)
- Focus session indicator — replaces High Priority metric card when
  a lock-in session is active, shows remaining minutes
- Standup export includes done/in-progress/todo/blockers sections

Major dashboard upgrade: metrics, activity feed, Cmd+K search, tags, glass UI

Inspired by builderz-labs/mission-control dashboard patterns:

- Add metric cards row: Todo, In Progress, Completed, High Priority counts
- Add activity feed right panel (collapsible with ] shortcut)
- Add Cmd+K search overlay with arrow key navigation + status badges
- Add tag support on tasks (colored pills, hash-based colors, comma input)
- Add loading shimmer skeleton while tasks load
- Add glass morphism header with backdrop-blur
- Add digital clock in header
- Add priority left border on cards (red/accent/gray by priority)
- Add custom scrollbar styling
- Add description line-clamp (2 lines, expand on focus)
- Add rose theme to PUT /api/config validation
- Add tags field to DashboardTask type + API handlers
- Better responsive: 2-col metrics on tablet, 1-col on mobile, feed hidden
- Activity log tracks add/move/delete/edit actions with colored dots

Polish dashboard: search, keyboard shortcuts, settings panel, rose theme

- Add search/filter bar (/ shortcut) to find tasks across columns
- Add keyboard shortcuts: N for new task, Esc to close, / to search
- Add settings panel (gear icon) with inline bot name + theme config
- Add PUT /api/config endpoint for settings changes without reload
- Add 4th theme: rose (warm pink)
- Add relative timestamps on cards (2h ago, 3d ago)
- Add task count in header (done/total)
- Add card fade-in animation on render
- Add paw favicon (data URI)
- Add per-column empty state messages
- Add mobile responsive breakpoint
- Enter key commits inline title edits

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump version to 1.5.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace focus timer paw art with braille cat duo animation

Two cats sitting together in braille dot art with breathing glow
animation. Replaced SVG ring with thin progress bar for cleaner layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump version to 1.5.0 — Lock In Mode with script generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove 80% window resizing from lockin start script

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge focus-mode: Lock In Mode with PAC blocking + script generation

- Renamed Focus Mode → Lock In Mode
- PAC file site blocking (no admin password needed)
- Shell script generation for fast execution (2 commands vs 12+)
- ASCII paw focus timer with progress ring
- Verified YouTube music presets
- Window auto-arrangement + dashboard timer

Fix YouTube presets: replace broken waterfall + mislabeled rain URLs

- Waterfall (lSTgq3bSXNI) was removed/unavailable → replaced with water stream sounds (jkLRith2wcc, 10h)
- Rain (eKFTSSKCzWA) was actually forest/waterfall → replaced with dedicated rain (jX6kn9_U8qk, 10h, 157M views)
- Added duration hints to all YouTube preset options

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Speed up lockin: generate shell scripts instead of 12+ individual commands

Claude now runs `openpaw lockin gen-scripts` to produce /tmp/lockin-start.sh
and /tmp/lockin-end.sh, then executes each with a single `bash` call.
Reduces lock-in from ~12 sequential tool calls to 2-3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

YouTube music: store URLs instead of search queries, just open in browser

Presets now map to actual YouTube URLs (lo-fi hip hop, rain, waterfall,
etc.) instead of search strings. Claude just runs `open "URL"` — no
yt-dlp download, no afplay, no temp files. User can also enter a
custom URL. Removes all download/cache logic from wizard and SKILL.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-start dashboard + pre-download YouTube audio in wizard

- Dashboard: add --no-open flag so SKILL.md can start it without
  auto-opening the main page in the browser
- SKILL.md: check if dashboard is running (curl :3141), start it
  with --no-open if not, then open the focus timer page
- Wizard: when user picks YouTube music, pre-download the audio file
  during setup so lock-in sessions start instantly (no download wait)
- SKILL.md: play cached audio from /tmp/lockin-audio.* if it exists,
  fall back to download-then-play if cache is missing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix site blocking, music, window positioning, lights wizard + fast tone

- Site blocking: set proxy on ALL network services instead of hardcoded
  "Wi-Fi" (user is on Ethernet/en0), remove broken Chrome URLBlocklist
  (only works from managed preferences, not user defaults)
- YouTube music: download to temp file then afplay (afplay can't read
  from stdin pipe, was silently failing)
- Window positioning: concrete AppleScript that calculates 80% screen
  bounds dynamically instead of CALCULATED_BOUNDS placeholder
- Timer: always try to open, position top-left, bring coding app back
- Lights wizard: always show (not gated on hasHue), offer openhue
  install via brew, handle missing rooms with manual entry
- SKILL.md tone: "be FAST — one line to start, execute silently, one
  line when done" instead of narrating every step

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enhance focus timer with ASCII paw animation and progress ring

Adds breathing ASCII paw art, walking paw trail animation, SVG progress
ring that fills as session progresses, rotating motivational quotes,
and a happy paw completion state. Three ambient glows for depth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace SelfControl with PAC file + Chrome policy site blocking

SelfControl required an admin password dialog on every session start.
PAC files + Chrome managed policies block sites with zero admin prompts
for admin-group users. Also positions dashboard timer at top-left.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SelfControl blocklist format + enforce sequential execution

SelfControl expects HostBlacklist (not Blocklist) with plain string
entries (not hostname dicts). Also added explicit "run steps ONE AT A
TIME" instructions to prevent parallel bash calls that cascade errors
when one fails. Fixed yt-dlp command to use -f bestaudio flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add dashboard focus timer page at /focus

New /focus route shows a minimal countdown timer during lock-in sessions.
Opens via `open http://localhost:3141/focus?ends=ISO&duration=MIN`.
Includes /api/focus endpoint for session data. Extracted theme colors to
shared constant to avoid duplication between dashboard and timer pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SelfControl integration + rewrite SKILL.md for direct execution

Detect SelfControl.app during setup, prompt to install via Homebrew if
missing. Site blocking now uses SelfControl (free, open-source, unbypassable)
instead of /etc/hosts. SKILL.md rewritten with comprehensive bash commands
for every feature — Claude runs commands directly instead of wrapping in
CLI subcommands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove lockin CLI subcommands — Claude runs commands directly

Remove start/end/status/auto-end subcommands and all action functions
from core (site blocking, app quitting, DND, music, lights, bluetooth,
window management, git stats, notifications). Keep only config/session
I/O, detectCapabilities, and the setup wizard. CLAUDE.md now points to
SKILL.md instead of `openpaw lockin start`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto window management, git commit messages in receipt, SKILL.md rewrite

- Window management is now automatic: detects frontmost app, centers it
  at 80% screen size, minimizes everything else. No wizard config needed.
- Session end receipt now includes actual git commit messages, not just
  counts. Auto-end Claude summary also gets commit messages.
- SKILL.md rewritten to use `openpaw lockin start` exclusively instead
  of individual bash commands (which fail without admin privileges).
- Removed windows field from LockInConfig and the window wizard section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix site blocking: replace sudo with osascript admin privileges

sudo fails in non-interactive contexts like Claude Code's bash tool.
Use osascript's native macOS GUI password dialog instead, which works
everywhere. Adds runAsAdmin() helper, rewrites blockSites, unblockSites,
startRoastServer, and stopRoastServer to use it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add custom roast page for blocked sites

When users visit blocked sites during a lock-in session, they see a
dark-themed page with a paw emoji, the site name, a random roast from
20 hardcoded messages, time remaining, and attempt counter — instead
of a generic browser error.

- Self-contained Node.js HTTP server on port 80 (requires sudo)
- Spawned as detached process, writes PID for cleanup
- Auto-terminates when session expires
- Tracks and increments blockedSiteAttempts in session file
- Cleaned up automatically on session end via stopRoastServer()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add window auto-arrangement to Lock In Mode

- New osascript functions: saveWindowPositions, arrangeWindows, restoreWindows
- Side-by-side layout (IDE left, terminal right) or stacked (IDE top 60%, terminal bottom 40%)
- Auto-detect running IDEs (VS Code, Cursor, Xcode, etc.) and terminals (iTerm2, Warp, Ghostty, etc.)
- Save window positions before arranging, restore on session end
- Minimize non-essential windows during lock-in
- Setup wizard prompts for IDE, terminal, and layout selection
- Added savedWindowPositions to LockInSession type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename Focus Mode → Lock In Mode across entire codebase

- Rename files: focus.ts → lockin.ts (commands + core), c-focus → c-lockin
- Rename types: FocusConfig → LockInConfig, FocusSession → LockInSession
- Rename functions: readFocusConfig → readLockInConfig, etc.
- CLI: `openpaw lockin` replaces `openpaw focus`
- Config: lockin.json replaces focus.json, # OPENPAW-LOCKIN marker
- Add windows field to LockInConfig type (prep for commit 2)
- Update all UI strings, SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge pull request #1 from daxaur/focus-mode

Add Focus Mode

commit.message:
Add paw walk animation between focus setup wizard steps

Little 🐾 walks across the terminal between each section —
sniffing system, distractions, apps, bluetooth, vibes, lights,
finishing up, and saving config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update setup outro to point to Claude instead of CLI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Ask where to install focus skill in setup wizard

Matches the main wizard pattern — global ~/.claude/skills/ (with hint
showing how many skills are already there), project .claude/skills/,
or custom path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use select options for light color instead of text input

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sync repo SKILL.md template with installed version

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make openpaw focus show config instead of starting a session

Claude orchestrates focus sessions, not the CLI. Now `openpaw focus`
shows your saved preferences and active session status. Tells you to
ask Claude to "focus" or "lock in" to start.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify SKILL.md to static template pointing to JSON

Single source of truth: the JSON config. SKILL.md is now a static
template that tells Claude to read ~/.config/openpaw/focus.json for
preferences and shows the commands for each feature. Setup writes
both files, configure only touches the JSON.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove JSON read instruction from generated SKILL.md

Preferences are already baked into the SKILL.md — Claude doesn't need
to read the JSON file. The JSON exists for the CLI commands (auto-end,
start, end, status), not for Claude.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove focus from skill catalog — setup handles everything

Focus Mode doesn't need `openpaw add focus` since it has no external
CLI tools to install. `openpaw focus setup` is the single entry point
that writes the config JSON and generates the personalized SKILL.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Generate personalized SKILL.md from focus setup wizard

When the user runs `openpaw focus setup`, the wizard now generates a
personalized SKILL.md at ~/.claude/skills/c-focus/ with their exact
preferences baked in — blocked sites, apps, music commands, bluetooth
device, lights, DND, etc. Claude reads one file with everything it
needs to orchestrate a focus session.

Also removes stale calendarBlock and telegramNotify from config init.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite SKILL.md: Claude orchestrates focus directly

Instead of wrapping everything in CLI commands, the SKILL.md now
gives Claude the exact shell commands to run for each step (block
sites, quit apps, connect bluetooth, play music, set lights, DND,
Slack). Claude reads the config, asks about ask-each-time items in
conversation, executes commands directly, and handles errors
naturally. CLI still exists for setup wizard and auto-end timer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-end focus session with Claude summary via Telegram

When focus starts, a background process sleeps for N minutes then
runs `openpaw focus auto-end`. This spawns a Haiku session via the
Claude Agent SDK that restores the environment, reads the git stats
receipt, writes an encouraging summary, and delivers it via Telegram
(or native notification as fallback). Same pattern as the scheduler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add non-interactive focus commands for Claude Code

Adds `openpaw focus start [--all]`, `openpaw focus end`, and
`openpaw focus status` — plain text output that Claude can read
and act on. Claude starts sessions, reads the receipt on end,
and summarizes the session naturally.

Updates SKILL.md with full instructions for Claude: when to use
start vs start --all, how to handle ask-each-time items in
conversation, and how to summarize the focus receipt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 6 bugs found in focus mode audit

- Remove calendarBlock (was never implemented, just a dead toggle)
- Remove telegramNotify (was calling local notification, not Telegram)
- Fix lights color placeholder submitting "warm" on empty enter
- Fix unused total variable in printConfig
- Fix sudo site blocking: use stdio inherit so user can enter password
- Remove dead url/local music source code paths
- Timer option only shown when terminal-notifier is detected

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add background timer that notifies when focus session ends

Uses detached spawn to sleep for N minutes then fire
terminal-notifier. Survives after the CLI process exits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix YouTube music: use ytsearch for preset queries

yt-dlp needs a URL or ytsearch: prefix — plain strings like
"white noise 1 hour" would fail. Now auto-prefixes ytsearch1:
for non-URL queries so presets actually work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Custom option to site picker + music presets per source

Websites: "Custom..." option in the multiselect — text input only
appears when selected. Music: preset playlists per source (lo-fi,
white noise, nature, rain, waterfall, brown noise, etc.) with a
Custom option for typing your own query/URL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Only show music option when a source is detected

Removed always-available "Open a URL" and "Local file" fallbacks.
Music prompt only appears if Spotify, Apple Music, Sonos, or yt-dlp
is detected on the machine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix custom sites input submitting placeholder as value

clack p.text returns placeholder on empty submit. Switched to
defaultValue: "" so hitting enter skips without adding sites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clean up site/app selection flow in focus setup

Simplified to 3 steps: select sites → add custom → mark which ask
each time. Removed twitter.com (x.com covers it). Same cleaner
flow for app quitting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove HN from default blocked sites list

Custom sites can still be added during setup via the comma-separated
text prompt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix focus setup: run detection before clack prompts

execSync calls (osascript, etc.) inside detectCapabilities() were
corrupting the terminal raw mode state, causing the duration text
input to glitch. Moving detection before p.intro() fixes it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add c-focus SKILL.md and Focus Mode section in CLAUDE.md

Creates skills/c-focus/SKILL.md with full usage instructions for Claude.
Updates CLAUDE.md generation to include Focus Mode section when configured,
so Claude knows to suggest `openpaw focus` when users express intent to
concentrate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wire focus into CLI, configure menu, and skill catalog

Adds `openpaw focus` (start), `openpaw focus setup`, and
`openpaw focus configure` commands. Adds Focus Mode option
to the configure menu. Registers c-focus in the skill catalog.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add focus command with setup wizard and session management

Setup wizard auto-detects machine capabilities (bluetooth devices, Spotify,
Hue rooms, Slack, Obsidian, etc.) and only shows relevant questions.
Supports website blocking (always vs ask-each-time), app quitting, bluetooth
auto-connect, music from 6 sources, Hue lights, DND, Slack DND, calendar
blocking, timer notifications, Obsidian logging, and Telegram notifications.
Focus start shows receipt with git stats on session end.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add FocusConfig type and core focus module

Adds auto-detection of machine capabilities (bluetooth, Spotify, Hue,
Slack, Sonos, etc.), config read/write, website blocking via /etc/hosts,
app quitting via osascript, DND, music control across 6 sources, git
stats tracking, Obsidian logging, and notification helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add model preferences to configure menu

openpaw configure → Model preferences now lets you:
- Change default Telegram model (sonnet/haiku/opus)
- Change default scheduling model
- Adjust daily cost cap
Updates CLAUDE.md after changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add smart scheduling to setup wizard

Wizard now offers to enable scheduling with cost control:
- Set daily cost cap
- Optionally create first recurring job inline
- Summary shows scheduling config
- CLAUDE.md includes scheduling section when configured

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update demo GIF, add dashboard screenshot, move GIF to top

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove ASCII art from README

The demo GIF serves as the visual header now.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add setup demo GIF to README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite README intro — cover the full picture

Explains identity, memory, permissions, safety hooks, Telegram,
dashboard, scheduling, and auth — not just skill downloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add proper intro section to README

Explains what OpenPaw is, how it works (SKILL.md files, CLI tools,
permissions), and what makes it different (no daemon, no cloud).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump to v1.4.0

- CLAUDE.md uses section markers (preserves existing content)
- Cleaned up README, fixed all broken repo links
- Interactive inline auth during setup and add

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix broken gog repo link in README

steipete/gog is also 404 — listed as plain text now.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

CLAUDE.md uses section markers, clean up README

- CLAUDE.md now uses <!-- OPENPAW:START/END --> markers so existing
  user content is preserved on setup/add/remove
- README rewritten: removed filler sections, fixed all broken repo
  links (nicklama/* were 404), removed linear-cli dead link
- Tools without public repos listed as plain text instead of links

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Interactive inline auth during setup and add

Instead of just showing auth reminders, the wizard and add command
now offer to run auth commands interactively (e.g. gh auth login,
spotify_player auth). Users can skip individual steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove broken tools: Things 3, editly, AI skill

Things 3 (pipx not available, no brew formula), editly (native gl
dependency fails to compile). Remove AI/LLM skill (unnecessary).
Sonoscli confirmed working. Update README skill counts to 38.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add configure command, clean up gitignore

Add `openpaw configure` (alias: config) — interactive menu to add
skills, edit personality, open dashboard, manage Telegram/schedules.
Add rr/ and *.tgz to gitignore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Agent identity via CLAUDE.md, fix dashboard add, better install UX

Generate ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md during setup so Claude knows its name,
installed skills, and dashboard. Regenerate on add/remove. Rewrite
dashboard HTML with DOM-based rendering (fixes task add bug). Show
individual skill install progress and final setup summary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

README: full paw ASCII art + 3D title, enable image refs

Replace small paw with the full detailed block-character paw art
and 3D OPENPAW title. Uncomment image references for demo.gif
and dashboard.png. Add docs/ directory for screenshots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.2.0: Task dashboard, README overhaul, shadow line fix

Add local kanban dashboard (3 themes: Paw/Midnight/Neon) with
drag-and-drop, inline editing, and JSON persistence on port 3141.
Dashboard integrated into setup wizard with theme picker.
Professional README with tool links, changelog, and architecture.
Fix shadow ░ bar alignment under 3D title. Bump to v1.2.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

True 3D ASCII title with extruded depth effect

- Letters separated by ░ depth chars instead of spaces
- Bottom ░ bar creates floor shadow
- Two-color render: main face (bright brown) + depth (dark brown)
- Shifted right to center under paw visual mass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

3D title shadow, overwrite protection, remove telegram-only mode

- Title art now renders with drop shadow (░ silhouette in dark brown)
- SOUL.md: asks before overwriting existing personality config
- Skills: asks before updating already-installed skill templates
- Remove "Telegram only" from wizard interface options (use openpaw telegram directly)
- Simplify dangerous mode + tmux launch logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ASCII title art + category labels in skill picker

- Replace box renderer with block-character OPENPAW title art
- Add category labels to first skill hint in each group
- Filter telegram from custom skill selection (handled by interface mode)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Single flat multiselect for skill picking with category icons

One clean screen — category icons (📝💬🎵🏠🔍⚡🤖⚙️) visually
group skills without using the glitchy groupMultiselect component.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace glitchy groupMultiselect with clean two-step flow

Step 1: Pick categories (8 clean options with skill counts)
Step 2: Pick individual skills from selected categories only

Avoids the rendering glitches from @clack/prompts groupMultiselect
with 39+ items. Each step is a standard multiselect — smooth and fast.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add prerequisite checks for brew and npm

Shows a clear note with install instructions when brew or npm are
missing, and asks the user whether to continue anyway.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

UX overhaul + 4 new assistant skills + bot naming

- Banner: single brown paw, no multicolor fade animation
- Wizard: consolidated skill selection into one grouped multiselect
- Wizard: removed MCP servers and working directory prompts
- Wizard: restored install location as user-modifiable
- Soul: users can now name their bot (default: Paw)
- New skills: weather, clipboard, contacts, timer/pomodoro (39 total)
- Updated README, catalog, and c-core skill table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add smart scheduling, video editing, Obsidian memory sync (v1.1.0)

- Smart Scheduling: `openpaw schedule add/list/remove/run/costs`
  - Human-readable schedules ("weekdays 8am", "every 30 min")
  - launchd (macOS) + cron (Linux) system integration
  - Built-in daily cost cap ($5 default) + per-run budgets
  - Deliver results to Telegram, file, or macOS notification
  - Cost tracking with 30-day history

- Video Editing: new c-video-edit skill
  - Remotion (React-based programmatic video creation)
  - Editly (JSON-based declarative video assembly)
  - Both tools installable via npm

- Obsidian Memory Sync: auto bidirectional sync
  - When both c-memory and c-obsidian installed, auto-sync
  - Memory files mirrored to Obsidian AI/ folder
  - Session logs appended to Obsidian daily notes

- 32 skills total (was 30)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 6 broken CLI tools, remove social skill, bump to v1.0.1

- memo: steipete/tap/memo → antoniorodr/memo/memo (correct tap)
- notion-cli: notion-cli-tool → @litencatt/notion-cli (package exists)
- icalpal: replaced with ical-buddy (brew install ical-buddy)
- bird: removed (deleted from steipete/tap), social skill removed
- openhue: brew install openhue-cli → brew install openhue/cli/openhue-cli
- slack-cli: brew tap compound cmd → npm install -g slack-cli
- things-cli: pip3 → pipx (fixes externally-managed-environment)
- README: fix npm badge to pawmode, update skill count to 30
- All 30 remaining tools verified installable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

OpenPaw v2: Telegram bridge, wizard overhaul, npm publish as pawmode

- Built-in Telegram bridge (grammy + Claude Agent SDK) — bidirectional,
  skills auto-map to bot /commands, model switching, streaming responses
- Wizard: interface mode (terminal/telegram/both), directory selection,
  puppy disclaimer for --dangerously-skip-permissions, tmux support
- PAW MODE indicator in c-core SKILL.md + SOUL.md
- Puppy personality throughout wizard strings
- Removed Discord skill (for now)
- README overhaul: puppy tone, no OpenClaw bashing, Telegram docs
- Published to npm as 'pawmode' (v1.0.0) — npx pawmode

New files: src/core/telegram.ts, src/core/tmux.ts, src/commands/telegram.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Telegram + Discord: curl-based, no daemon needed

- c-telegram: Bot API via curl — send messages, read updates, send files
  Setup: create bot via @BotFather, save token to ~/.config/openpaw/telegram.env
- c-discord: Webhooks via curl — send messages, embeds, files
  Setup: create webhook in channel settings, save URL
- Removed fake tg/discord-cli npm packages from catalog
- Both skills use only curl + jq (already on most systems)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add MCP server setup + enhance Obsidian memory integration

- New src/core/mcp.ts: catalog of 10 MCP servers (filesystem, fetch,
  memory, github, slack, gdrive, postgres, brave, puppeteer, thinking)
- Wizard step 8b: optional MCP server selection with env var reminders
- Enhanced c-obsidian SKILL.md: daily notes, session logging, memory sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add soul, export, and import subcommands

- openpaw soul: standalone personality editor (create/overwrite SOUL.md)
- openpaw export: bundle skills, permissions, soul, memory to JSON
- openpaw import <file>: restore configuration on another machine

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add daily briefing skill + skill dependency resolution

c-briefing compiles morning summaries from installed skills (email,
calendar, tasks, etc). Includes instructions for scheduling via cron.

Added `depends` field to Skill type — when installing c-briefing,
auto-includes c-email and c-calendar as dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Telegram + Discord skills to catalog

New skills: c-telegram (tg CLI) and c-discord (discord CLI).
Both support sending/reading messages, groups, file sharing.
Added to Communication category in the catalog.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update c-core coordinator — memory integration, OpenPaw identity, session checklist

Coordinator now instructs Claude to read SOUL.md and MEMORY.md at
session start, save facts to memory, and identify as OpenPaw-powered.
Updated skill table to include all 33 skills.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wizard overhaul — SOUL.md step, category-by-category selection, launch Claude

- Personality questionnaire (SOUL.md) added as step 2
- Memory directory auto-setup during wizard
- Custom skill selection now walks category by category with icons
- Paw emoji loading spinners during installation
- Offers to launch Claude Code after setup is complete
- c-memory skill always installed alongside c-core

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add c-memory skill + memory directory setup

SKILL.md teaches Claude to read/write persistent memory in
~/.claude/memory/ — MEMORY.md for key facts, plus topic files
for people, preferences, projects, and a session journal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SOUL.md personality system — questionnaire + writer

Interactive questionnaire asks name, tone, verbosity, proactivity,
and custom instructions. Generates ~/.claude/SOUL.md that Claude
reads at session start for personalized behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Brown paw art with boxed title — professional banner

Uses the detailed graduated block paw art (▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█) in warm brown
(#b4783c). Title displayed in a centered box below the paw. Fade-in
animation with brown color ramp. Removes gradient-string dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Graduated block paw art + fix CI typecheck errors

Paw art now uses ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█ graduated blocks for smooth anti-aliased
edges (same technique as the user's reference art). Single teal color,
21 lines, professional look.

Fixes: import ChalkInstance type properly (was chalk.ChalkInstance
which doesn't exist in chalk v5). Remove unused gradient-string import
that caused TS4023 errors with AliasGradientFunction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clean single-color paw art — static block characters, no truecolor engine

Replaces the procedural truecolor pixel art engine with simple static
block art (▀ ▄ █) colored with a single chalk.hex() call per mood.
Fade-in animation via 3 brightness steps. No screen clear, no readline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

releases.name: v1.5.0 — Lock In Mode
releases.body:
## Lock In Mode 🔒

A complete focus session orchestrator. Tell Claude to "lock in" and it sets up your entire environment in seconds — blocks distracting sites, quits apps, sets the mood, and tracks your work.

### What's New

**Lock In Mode** (renamed from Focus Mode)
- **Site blocking via PAC files** — blocks sites across all browsers with zero admin password dialogs. Works on Wi-Fi, Ethernet, any network interface.
- **Chrome incognito disabled** during sessions to prevent bypass
- **App quitting** — auto-quit distracting apps (Messages, Discord, etc.)
- **YouTube music presets** — verified long-duration links: Lo-fi Girl livestream, white noise (10h), rain sounds (10h), brown noise (8h), water/stream sounds (10h). Or paste any YouTube URL.
- **Hue lights** — set room, brightness, and color. Wizard offers to install openhue if missing.
- **Bluetooth auto-connect** — connect headphones when session starts
- **macOS Do Not Disturb** — silence notifications automatically
- **Slack DND** — set Slack to DND for the session duration
- **Timer notification** — native macOS alert when session ends

**Focus Timer Dashboard**
- New `/focus` page on the dashboard (localhost:3141) with:
  - ASCII paw art with breathing animation
  - SVG progress ring that fills as session progresses
  - Rotating motivational quotes
  - Completion state with celebration animation
  - Theme support (paw / midnight / neon)

**Script Generation for Speed**
- `openpaw lockin gen-scripts` generates a single shell script from your config
- Claude runs **2 bash commands** instead of 12+ sequential ones
- Sessions start in seconds, not minutes

**Session Tracking**
- Git commit count + diff stats at session end
- Optional Obsidian logging
- Session file at `~/.config/openpaw/lockin-session.json`

### Setup

```bash
openpaw lockin setup
```

Interactive wizard auto-detects your tools (Spotify, Hue, Bluetooth, etc.) and only asks relevant questions.

Then just tell Claude: **"lock in"**

### No Extra Dependencies

The core features (site blocking, app quitting, DND, window management, timer) use only macOS built-ins. No extra CLIs needed unless you opt into Hue lights, Bluetooth, Spotify, or Sonos.

releases.name: v1.4.0
releases.body:
## What's New

### CLAUDE.md Section Markers
OpenPaw no longer overwrites `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`. It now uses `<!-- OPENPAW:START -->` / `<!-- OPENPAW:END -->` markers to insert/update only its own section. Your existing instructions are preserved.

### Interactive Auth
The setup wizard and `openpaw add` now offer to run auth commands inline (e.g. `gh auth login`, `spotify_player auth`) instead of just showing reminders.

### Cleanup
- README rewritten — shorter, no filler, all repo links verified
- Removed broken tool links (nicklama/*, steipete/gog, linear/linear-cli)
- Removed broken tools from catalog (things-cli, editly)
- Removed unnecessary AI/LLM skill

## Full Changelog
https://github.com/daxaur/openpaw/compare/v1.2.0...v1.4.0

releases.name: v1.2.0
releases.body:
## What's New

- **Task Dashboard** — local kanban board with 3 themes (Paw, Midnight, Neon), drag-and-drop, runs on `localhost:3141`
- **3D ASCII Title** — extruded block-character title with depth effect
- **Overwrite Protection** — existing SOUL.md and skills are preserved unless you choose to update
- **4 New Skills** — Weather, Clipboard, Contacts, Timer
- **Dependency Resolution** — skills that need other skills auto-install them
- **Professional README** — tool links, changelog, architecture docs

## Install

```bash
npx pawmode
```

readme_html:
What is OpenPaw?

Claude Code is powerful, but out of the box it only knows how to code. OpenPaw turns it into a full personal assistant — one that reads your email, plays your music, controls your lights, manages your calendar, tracks your packages, and remembers what you told it last week.

Run one command and OpenPaw:

  • Installs 38 skills across email, calendar, Spotify, smart home, Slack, GitHub, Obsidian, and more
  • Sets up the CLI tools each skill needs (via Homebrew, npm, or pip) and walks you through auth
  • Gives Claude an identity — a name, personality, and persistent memory across sessions
  • Configures permissions and safety hooks so Claude can act autonomously without doing anything dangerous
  • Adds a Telegram bridge so you can talk to Claude from your phone
  • Includes a task dashboard — a local kanban board with drag-and-drop and 3 themes
  • Enables smart scheduling — recurring tasks with per-run and daily cost caps so nothing runs away
  • Can paw-style Claude Code itself — branded colors, spinners, input highlighting, and chat styling via openpaw theme

After setup, you just talk to Claude:

"Check my email and summarize anything urgent"
"Play lo-fi beats on Spotify"
"Turn the bedroom lights to 20%"
"What's on my calendar tomorrow?"
"Add milk to my reminders"
"Go to Hacker News and summarize the top 5 posts"

Claude figures out which skill to use. No special syntax needed.

No daemon, no cloud, no subscriptions. OpenPaw runs once, writes config, and gets out of the way. Everything runs locally through your existing Claude Code subscription.

Quick Start

npx pawmode

The wizard walks you through picking skills, choosing your interface (terminal, Telegram, or both), setting up a task dashboard, and authenticating with services. Or skip the wizard entirely:

npx pawmode --preset essentials       # common skills, no prompts
npx pawmode --preset developer --yes  # fully non-interactive

Skills

38 skills across 8 categories. Install only what you need.

Productivity

Skill What it does Tools
c-notes Apple Notes + Reminders memo, remindctl
c-obsidian Obsidian vault management obsidian-cli
c-notion Notion pages + databases notion-cli
c-tasks Todoist / Taskwarrior todoist, taskwarrior

Communication

Skill What it does Tools
c-email Gmail or IMAP — read, send, search gog, himalaya
c-calendar Google Calendar + Apple Calendar gog, icalBuddy
c-messaging iMessage + WhatsApp imsg, wacli
c-slack Slack channels + DMs slack-cli
c-telegram Telegram bridge (built-in) —

Media

Skill What it does Tools
c-music Spotify playback + search spogo
c-video YouTube download + convert yt-dlp, ffmpeg
c-video-edit Programmatic video creation remotion
c-screen Screenshots, OCR, webcam peekaboo, camsnap
c-voice Speech-to-text + TTS sag

Smart Home

Skill What it does Tools
c-lights Philips Hue control openhue-cli
c-speakers Sonos speakers sonoscli
c-bluetooth Bluetooth devices blueutil

Browser & Automation

Skill What it does Tools
c-browser Headless browser — navigate, click, scrape playwright
c-cron Cron jobs + launchctl services lunchy
c-schedule Recurring tasks with cost control built-in
c-briefing Daily morning briefing depends on email + calendar

System & Files

Skill What it does Tools
c-system Volume, wifi, battery, dock m-cli
c-apps Mac App Store from CLI mas
c-files Cloud sync (Drive, S3, Dropbox, 70+) rclone
c-display Brightness + safe trash brightness, macos-trash
c-notify Native macOS notifications terminal-notifier
c-clipboard Copy, paste, transform clipboard built-in
c-contacts Search macOS Contacts built-in
c-timer Countdown, alarms, pomodoro terminal-notifier

Research & Utilities

Skill What it does Tools
c-weather Weather forecasts built-in (curl + wttr.in)
c-research Summarize URLs, PDFs, videos summarize
c-location Apple Maps + nearby places goplaces
c-tracking Package tracking (UPS, FedEx, etc.) ordercli
c-secrets 1Password / Bitwarden op, bw
c-network DNS + HTTP client doggo, httpie

Developer

Skill What it does Tools
c-github PRs, issues, repos, actions gh, jq
c-linear Linear issues + projects linear
c-jira Jira issues + sprints jira-cli

Presets

Preset Skills
Everything All 38 skills
Essentials Email, calendar, notes, music, weather, clipboard, browser, system, notifications
Productivity Notes, Obsidian, tasks, email, calendar, Slack, cloud files, notifications
Developer GitHub, Linear, Jira, browser, network, cron
Creative Music, video, screen, voice, browser, research
Smart Home Lights, speakers, Bluetooth, system, display, notifications

Features

Telegram Bridge — Talk to Claude from your phone. Set up with openpaw telegram setup, start with openpaw telegram.

Task Dashboard — Local kanban board with 3 themes (Paw, Midnight, Neon). Run openpaw dashboard.

Scheduling — Recurring tasks with per-run and daily cost caps. openpaw schedule add "weekdays 8am" --run "check email".

Claude Code Paw Theme — Patch Claude Code with OpenPaw styling using tweakcc plus a native mascot fallback patch. Run openpaw theme install and then openpaw theme verify, or opt into it during openpaw setup. If your Claude Code build accepts custom themes, switch inside Claude Code with /theme openpaw; otherwise you still get the Paw mascot, welcome copy, mascot colors, lock-in status line, spinner, and input styling.

CLAUDE.md Integration — OpenPaw writes a section into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md so Claude knows its name, installed skills, and personality on every session start. Your existing CLAUDE.md content is preserved.

Commands

Command Description
openpaw Interactive setup wizard
openpaw configure Menu to add skills, edit personality, manage dashboard
openpaw add <skills> Add skills — openpaw add notes music email
openpaw remove <skills> Remove skills
openpaw list Show all available skills
openpaw status Show installed skills + tool versions
openpaw doctor Diagnose issues
openpaw update Upgrade CLI tools
openpaw dashboard Task dashboard (--theme midnight, --port 8080)
openpaw theme install Install the OpenPaw Claude Code patch globally
openpaw theme verify Verify that Claude Code contains OpenPaw mascot/theme markers
openpaw telegram Start Telegram bridge
openpaw schedule add Add a scheduled job
openpaw schedule list List scheduled jobs
openpaw schedule costs View cost usage
openpaw soul Edit personality
openpaw export / import Backup and restore config
openpaw reset Remove everything OpenPaw installed

Contributing

gh repo fork daxaur/openpaw
mkdir skills/c-yourskill
# Write a SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tags)
# Add tool definitions to src/catalog/index.ts
gh pr create

License

MIT — @daxaur
